title: Cloudflare Origin-Bypass Prevention — tunnel vs allowlist+AOP for the Cloudflare→AWS path
description: A workload is documented as 'behind Cloudflare' while its AWS origin (ALB/NLB or service) still accepts direct public traffic. Hulumi's Edge Platform (v1.3.2) enforces the cross-provider invariant that public application traffic reaches AWS origins only through Cloudflare-controlled paths: CloudflareOriginIngress offers a tunnel mode (no public origin at all) or an allowlist+AOP mode (origin security group restricted to Cloudflare ranges plus Authenticated Origin Pull). CF_ORIGIN_1 requires secure-origin evidence; the origin-bypass pack's X_ORIGIN_1 reports public AWS origins reachable around Cloudflare.
actors: AI Coding Agent (trusted; may create ALB and Cloudflare DNS in separate code paths)
External Attacker (untrusted; discovers and connects directly to the origin)
Contributor (semi-trusted; may pick public origin mode for convenience)
assets: Cloudflare Tunnel configuration or Authenticated Origin Pull material
The public application served through Cloudflare

stride.name: Origin-bypass breach via direct origin reachability
stride.description: An attacker discovers the ALB DNS name or a historical origin IP (DNS history, leaked IP, DNS-only record) and connects directly to the AWS origin, bypassing Cloudflare WAF, bot controls, rate limiting, and Access. Mitigation: CloudflareOriginIngress defaults to tunnel mode (no public origin) or allowlist+AOP; CF_ORIGIN_1 requires secure-origin evidence and X_ORIGIN_1 reports a public AWS origin reachable around Cloudflare.

stride.name: Spoofed origin requests not from Cloudflare
stride.description: Even with a restricted security group, an attacker inside an allowed network path could reach the origin if requests are not authenticated as coming from Cloudflare. Mitigation: allowlist+AOP mode requires Authenticated Origin Pull (mTLS client certificate) so the origin trusts only Cloudflare; tunnel mode removes inbound exposure entirely.

stride.name: Security group widened back to 0.0.0.0/0
stride.description: The origin security group is changed out of band to accept public ingress again, re-opening the bypass. Mitigation: allowlist+AOP pins Cloudflare IP ranges in the security group as managed state; X_ORIGIN_1 is the fixture-backed cross-provider check that flags a public AWS origin alongside a Cloudflare public-app record.

stride.name: Public origin mode selected without source restrictions
stride.description: A contributor selects a public origin mode without Cloudflare source restrictions to make a deployment easier, weakening origin posture. Mitigation: a discriminated-union argument prevents mixed insecure modes and an explicit acknowledgePublicOrigin path is required before any public origin mode is accepted.

stride.name: No evidence of chosen mode or compensating controls
stride.description: Without recorded evidence of the chosen ingress mode, an auditor cannot tell whether the cross-provider invariant holds. Mitigation: CloudflareOriginIngress emits outputs documenting the chosen mode and compensating controls; CF_ORIGIN_1 only accepts ingress evidence whose hostname matches the public app record.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/platform-patterns.CloudflareOriginIngress
recommendedComponents.rationale: Tunnel mode (no public origin) or allowlist+AOP mode (Cloudflare-range security group plus Authenticated Origin Pull). Discriminated-union args refuse public origin mode without explicit controls.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/policies.cloudflare.HulumiCloudflareHardeningPack
recommendedComponents.availability: Shipped in @hulumi/policies@1.3.2 (Edge Platform; rule IDs stable)
recommendedComponents.rationale: Mandatory CF_ORIGIN_1 requires application hostnames to carry CloudflareOriginIngress tunnel or allowlist+AOP evidence whose hostname matches the public app record.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/policies.HulumiOriginBypassPack
recommendedComponents.availability: Shipped in @hulumi/policies@1.3.2 (Edge Platform; advisory/evolving until origin-bypass M4 sandbox fixtures)
recommendedComponents.rationale: Cross-provider X_ORIGIN_1 reports Cloudflare public-app records targeting an AWS ELB origin without tunnel or allowlist+AOP evidence; advisory until sandbox fixture coverage promotes it.
